title: PrincipleEntry
description: A governing principle extracted from a promoted AEP governance decision. Principles are immutable once registered; arbitration outcome is recorded via PrincipleResolutionRecord rather than by modifying the principle. The `lifecycle_state` field is the only mutable field: it advances through the lifecycle state machine as arbitration and explicit lifecycle management events occur.

principle_id.description: Content-addressed identifier derived from principle_class, scope_descriptor, and source_entry_id. Two identical principles produce the same ID, making duplicates detectable.

principle_class.description: Taxonomy hierarchy classification. Lower level number = higher authority. LEVEL_0 always overrides LEVEL_3 when constraints collide. The hierarchy: LEVEL_0 (determinism/integrity invariants) > LEVEL_1 (safety boundaries) > LEVEL_2 (simulation accuracy) > LEVEL_3 (behavioral optimization heuristics) > LEVEL_4 (preference/style guidance).

scope_descriptor.description: Human-readable specificity annotation describing the scope of the governing rule. Narrower (more qualified) rules have longer, more specific descriptors. Used by the SPECIFICITY_COLLISION resolution path: the principle with the longer scope_descriptor wins within the same taxonomy level.

source_entry_id.description: entry_id of the AepDecisionLedgerEntry whose PROMOTED verdict produced this principle. Creates a traceable link from the active governing principle back to the governance event that justified it.

tolerance_delta.description: Optional numeric tolerance contribution. When a principle permits a floating-point tolerance (e.g. +0.003 coordinate drift), record it here so aggregateEnvelopeCheck() can sum contributions across all ACTIVE principles and guard against cumulative drift breaching the global invariant envelope (MAX_AGGREGATE_TOLERANCE = 0.01).

lifecycle_state.description: Current lifecycle state of this principle. ACTIVE: currently governing execution. SUBORDINATED: temporarily muted by a higher-priority overlapping constraint; reversible via reactivatePrinciple(). SUPERSEDED: permanently replaced by a newer content-addressed principle. DEPRECATED: frozen for legacy replay only; blocked from new proposals. RETIRED: administratively removed from all future consideration. Only ACTIVE principles contribute to execution governance. Only ACTIVE principles are counted by aggregateEnvelopeCheck().

superseded_by.description: Optional principle_id of the principle that superseded this one. Only set when lifecycle_state is SUPERSEDED. Forward lineage edge: enables traversal from ancestor to successor through the replacement chain.

supersedes.description: Optional principle_id of the principle that this principle directly superseded. Set on the newer (replacing) principle when supersedePrinciple() is called with a supersedingPrincipleId. Backward lineage edge: enables traversal from successor back to ancestor, complementing the superseded_by forward pointer. Together they form a doubly-linked replacement chain across principle generations.

lifecycle_catalyst.description: Catalyst that drove the most recent lifecycle state transition out of ACTIVE. EMPIRICAL: triggered by runtime arbitration or simulation evidence (SUBORDINATED from resolveConflict; SUPERSEDED from supersedePrinciple). ADMINISTRATIVE: triggered by deliberate maintainer policy action (DEPRECATED from deprecatePrinciple; RETIRED from retirePrinciple). Absent when lifecycle_state is ACTIVE. Cleared when a SUBORDINATED principle is reactivated. Preserved permanently for terminal states.
